column2: Which credible news channel, in 1957, convinced its viewers that 'spaghetti' can be grown on trees?

Which town, also serving as the capital of Easter Island, is home to nearly all its inhabitants?
How do we better know the "Antoinette Perry Award for Excellence in Broadway Theatre"?
What is the location of the World Health Organization's headquarters?
Where did Lord Buddha attain Nirvana?
Which is the only bird capable of flying backwards?
Which popular soft drink was named Brad's Drink when founded in 1893?
Which famous book opens with “It was a bright cold day in April, and the clocks were striking thirteen.”?
Which is the body's largest and strongest tendon connecting calf muscles to the heel bone?
Which US President was also the founder of the 207 years old University of Virginia?
Which northeastern Indian state celebrates Rongali Bihu?
Which country sponsored Christopher Colombus's transatlantic voyages?
George R.R. Martin's 'A Song of Ice and Fire' book series is primarily inspired by which English civil war?
